title: AMC Theatres Q2 2024 Earnings: Analyzing the $32M Loss
text:
AMC Q2 2024: Theater Recovery Hampered by Weak Film Slate

AMC Theatres reported Q2 2024 results showing challenging conditions due to a lighter film slate during the quarter. The theater chain reported a loss of $32.8 million as attendance and revenues declined significantly year-over-year.

Quarterly Performance: Total revenues were $1,030.6 million, representing a 23.5% decrease from Q2 2023's $1,347.9 million. The revenue decline stemmed directly from lower attendance driven by fewer blockbuster releases. Revenue per patron remained relatively stable, indicating pricing remained consistent.

Attendance Crisis: Global attendance of 50.0 million represented a 24.6% decline year-over-year. Admissions revenue fell 24.1% to $564.4 million, and food and beverage revenue dropped 24.8% to $367.1 million. The simultaneous decline in both metrics indicated comprehensive weakness across the business.

EBITDA Compression: Adjusted EBITDA compressed significantly to $29.4 million from $182.5 million in the prior year quarter, showing the operating leverage working in reverse during weak periods.

Industry Context: The second quarter of 2024 saw fewer blockbusters than in 2023, creating headwinds for the entire theater industry. However, success of Deadpool & Wolverine late in the quarter provided early indication of stronger momentum for the second half of 2024.
